hero.eyebrow: AIOS · A Local AI Hub for Real Spaces
hero.title: A space that understands you -- before you speak.
hero.footnote: AIOS Care | care facilities & operators    ·    AIOS Home | households & residences
hero.ctaSecondary: See it inside both kinds of spaces

era.title: Compute is coming back to you.
era.subtitle: For a decade we handed everything to the cloud. The next decade brings the most personal part back under your own roof.
era.col1Body: Inference returns to a place you can see, a plug you can pull.
era.col2Body: Before you speak, it is already watching, listening, preparing.
era.col3Body: An intelligence grown for you, your family, your space -- no one else.

lines.title:
One local hub.
Two kinds of spaces.
lines.subtitle: Eldercare and homes both look like "a space that understands you" -- but they are not the same thing. One side wants to avoid incidents; the other wants to be remembered.
lines.careTagline: Sold to fewer incidents -- and to being able to explain the ones that happen
lines.careBody: An elder gets out of bed at night. The system decides whether to call someone, routes it to the on-duty staff member's phone, and keeps the whole thread on record. This is the line we are actually pushing in the near term.
lines.carePoint1: Bed-leaving and falls -- the system tells what is serious, no alert flood
lines.carePoint2: Tickets routed to a person; who took it, how fast, all on record
lines.carePoint3: Family, staff, operator -- each sees what they should, no more
lines.homeTagline: Don't ask you to adapt to it -- it grows into the shape of your home
lines.homeBody: Not another box that talks back, but a local AI that quietly learns this household -- who runs cold, who sleeps light, when the living room should dim. Data never leaves home.
lines.homePoint1: Remembers who likes what, who runs on what schedule -- you don't keep explaining
lines.homePoint2: Reads the home without putting cameras everywhere
lines.homePoint3: Suggests first, acts second; the consequential moves always ask you
lines.homeCta: See how it learns your home
lines.footnote:
AIOS Care answers "how can a care space be safer and more efficient?";
AIOS Home answers "how can a home truly understand me?"

nightCare.title:
AIOS Care on the floor.
One night shift. One bed leaving. One record.
nightCare.subtitle: This is what AIOS Care looks like on the floor of an eldercare or community-care site -- not a talking box, but an acceptable event stream. The same record, seen by the resident, the system, the staff, the family and the operator, each from their own angle.

spectrum.title: From a bedside lamp, to a city block.
spectrum.subtitle: One kernel, many scales. Wherever you are, intelligence centers on you.
spectrum.bedsideBody: The first light, that understands your night.
spectrum.deskBody: The room prepared, before the thought arrives.
spectrum.homeBody: A house that understands the people living in it.
spectrum.communityBody: A building, a street -- looking out for each other, without looking in.

intelligence.title: Not reaction. Understanding.
intelligence.subtitle: AIOS Home is not about being able to talk -- it is about truly understanding this home. Not what it can answer, but that it already knows you.
intelligence.card1Body: It sees you walk into the room, before you speak.
intelligence.card2Body: It remembers how you turned off the lights last night.
intelligence.card3Body: It knows why, not just what.
intelligence.card4Body: One intent, the whole room responds.
intelligence.cta: Meet the intelligence that is yours

vision.title: The next computing platform isn't in your pocket. It's in the room.
vision.body: Phones are personal AI. The space is the new platform we are building. It starts from sensing, finishes in your home, earns your trust, and stays open to extend. Every room grows an intelligence kernel of its own.

platform.title: Seven years of engineering, one foundation.
platform.subtitle: Runs locally. Earns your trust. Stays open to extend.
platform.sensingBody: Five senses at once -- it reads the room like a person, not a single camera feed.
platform.edgeBody: All thinking happens inside your home. No round trips to the cloud; even offline, the essentials stay on.
platform.memoryBody: From the moment to the long-term, one layer at a time, it actually remembers you.
platform.protocolsBody: Works with mainstream ecosystems and protocols. No forced rebrand, no rip-and-replace.
platform.privacyBody: Data stays home by default; when the network drops, the essentials keep running.

trust.title: Every step leaves a trace anyone can check.
trust.body: Every thought, every action, every rule change is written down and cannot be quietly rewritten. Families, managers, regulators each see their own view -- no more, no less.

cta.title: Bring the thinking space into the next decade.

hero.title: Yours. Only yours.
hero.subtitle: Your habits, your rhythm, your home -- understood, never shared.

memory.title: Your Own Memory
memory.body: Preferences, habits, rhythms -- all modeled locally. This memory belongs to you alone. Never merged, never uploaded, never trained on.

personalization.title: Personalized Setup
personalization.body: A separate profile per family member. Grandparents' routines, kids' schedules, your commute -- never crossing, never interrupting.

learning.title: Adaptive Learning
learning.body: 30-day adaptive modeling. From the first light, the first cup of water -- it grows into you. No setup, no training.

orchestra.title: Cross-Device Orchestra
orchestra.body: Brand-agnostic, protocol-agnostic. One phrase, one gesture, one intent -- the whole home answers like an orchestra, not a wall of switches.

anticipatory.title: Anticipatory
anticipatory.body: Alerts that arrive before the incident. Care that arrives before the call. A fall can be caught early. A need can be met early.

privacy.title: Privacy Shield
privacy.body: Four-layer network lock plus default-zero egress allowlist. Faces, vitals, room video -- raw data never leaves your home.

offline.title: Offline-Capable
offline.body: If the network drops, the lights still turn on, the doors still lock, the alarm still reaches help. Critical capabilities always run locally.

household.title: Household Intelligence
household.body: One family, one memory. Shareable with loved ones, hideable from guests, passable to the next generation when elders are gone.

cta.title: Bring this intelligence into your space.

hero.title: A space is held up by many roles, together.
hero.subtitle: AIOS is not a single box. It is a system that divides labor -- a mind at the center, eyes and ears across the room, a few quiet ways to talk back, a polyglot layer of protocols, and finally the hands that carry out the action.

closing.title: Why five roles, not one product.
closing.body: A truly intelligent space should not be carried by one device alone. It needs a mind that thinks, senses that read the whole room, a few considered ways to talk back, a network that embraces what is already installed, and hands that can actually act. Only when each plays its part does a space that understands you become solid.
closing.bullet1: The mind thinks on-site. No cloud, no far-away.
closing.bullet2: Sensing never stares at you. It reads the whole room.
closing.bullet3: Traces the system leaves can be checked by family and managers alike.

hero.title: A space that understands you, stays open for others to extend.
hero.subtitle: Every plugin gives a room one more thing it can do. All run locally, all stay auditable, all belong only to your home.

cta.title: Want to give your plugin to this space?
cta.body: We have full developer docs, a sample project, and a ship-to-store path ready for builders. From a story to an auditable manifest, we walk it with you.

solutions.title: Solutions
solutions.subtitle: The same local AI hub, sitting in an eldercare site and sitting in a home, is not the same thing. One side wants fewer incidents; the other wants to be remembered. AIOS pursues both -- on two separate tracks.

health.title: AIOS Care · Eldercare and Care Sites
health.body: This is the line we are actually pushing right now. Home-based eldercare, community care, clinical assistance -- in these spaces, when something happens, someone is responsible. The system has to be able to explain what it did.

home.title: AIOS Home · Households and Residences
home.body: This line moves slower, and runs longer. It begins with high-end residences, designers and developer showcases, then extends to ordinary households. The pitch is not another smart speaker -- it is "this home gradually remembers me".

commercial.title: Commercial Spaces (In Labs)
commercial.body: Hotel, office, retail -- on the same AIOS kernel, validated in Labs. Engaged after the Care and Home delivery packages prove repeatable.

public.title: Education & Public (In Labs)
public.body: Campuses and cultural venues -- on the same AIOS kernel, in Labs validation. Outside the phase-one commercial commitment.

elderlyCare.title: Elderly Care
elderlyCare.brief: Falls, prolonged lying, bed-leaving, night patrol

hospitality.title: Hospitality
hospitality.brief: Rooms that move from buttons to understanding guests

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.brief: Meeting rooms that know the next step

retail.title: Retail Space
retail.brief: Shelves that see, spaces that read the flow

campus.title: Campus
campus.brief: Ambient intelligence for classrooms and dorms

products.title: Products
products.subtitle: Three forms, one kernel -- from a single home, to a building, to a whole hospital.

homeBox.name: Home Box
homeBox.desc: A local intelligence hub that lives in your home. All thinking happens on it, and all data stays with it.
homeBox.bullet1: Unboxed and running. Half a day to bring your devices together.
homeBox.bullet2: The lights, locks, and sensors you already own -- they can all join in.
homeBox.bullet3: Every decision stays on-device. Offline, the essentials keep running.

communityKit.name: Community Kit
communityKit.tag: For a building, for a community
communityKit.desc: Designed for senior apartments, community care centers, and properties of 20-200 units. Families see their own door, staff see the whole building -- each their own view.
communityKit.bullet1: One unit serves dozens to hundreds of homes. No far-away cloud.
communityKit.bullet2: Families, managers, operators -- each sees only their own view.
communityKit.bullet3: Ready for MLPS 2.0 audit and compliance flows.

enterpriseStack.name: Enterprise Stack
enterpriseStack.desc: On-prem deployment for top hospitals, specialty clinics, and large care facilities. Data stays inside, working alongside your existing systems.
enterpriseStack.bullet1: 7x24 dedicated support, customized alongside the department
enterpriseStack.bullet2: Full, exportable audit trace that plugs into your compliance flow
enterpriseStack.bullet3: Plugs into the hospital systems you already run -- no rip-and-replace

trust.title: Trust & Compliance
trust.subtitle: Local-First is not a slogan. It is a four-layer network lock, hash-chain audit, tiered RBAC and compliance mapping.

localFirst.title: Defense in Depth, Engineered
localFirst.body: Applied as a standard enterprise pattern: application allowlist, egress audit, container isolation, host firewall — four layers of least-privilege networking, with critical paths remaining online when disconnected.

auditChain.title: AuditLedger · Append-only Audit
auditChain.body: Every LLM inference, every execution, every rule change is appended to a local ledger and exportable as a compliance report.

rbac.title: RBAC · Roles and Multi-site Isolation
rbac.body: Standard role model (admin / operator / observer / user) with multi-site and floor-level access scopes — each person sees only what their role covers.

compliance.title: Aligned with MLPS / HIPAA / GDPR
compliance.body: Selected control-point mapping against MLPS 2.0 L3, HIPAA-like, and GDPR-like; the full matrix ships with the procurement brief. Audit logs export as JSONL, CSV, or PDF.

manifesto.title: Bringing inference back to you.
manifesto.p1: For the last decade we handed more and more to the cloud. It amplified what we could do, and quietly took some things with it -- our latency, our privacy, our sense of how these systems actually work.
manifesto.p2: We think the part of intelligence closest to you should stay close -- on a device you can see, in a place you can unplug. It won't always be the fastest option, but it should be the one you trust.
manifesto.p3: What SidebyStar is building is more than a product. It is a foundation we hope others can build on, so that ambient intelligence can grow naturally in every home, clinic and community.
manifesto.predictions: Three things we think will happen
manifesto.pred1: Every home will have a local intelligence hub, the way every home today has a router.
manifesto.pred2: Being traceable and auditable will become a baseline expectation of AI -- the way HTTPS is today.
manifesto.pred3: More collaboration between devices will happen locally, instead of routing through the cloud.

frontiers.title: What we're researching
frontiers.subtitle: What we've shipped, what is in the lab, and what we hope becomes a standard by 2030.

jepa.title: Visual models that can anticipate
jepa.body: Not only seeing what is happening, but sensing what might happen -- so that an alert can arrive before an incident.

causal.title: Memory that understands causes
causal.body: Going beyond event logs. We record why something happened, and how to avoid it next time.

endSideLlm.title: Small, intimate, on-device models
endSideLlm.body: Lightweight models running directly on local hardware in homes and facilities -- a kernel of intelligence that belongs to that space.

zkAudit.title: Provable compliance, no raw data shared
zkAudit.body: Let facilities prove to regulators that they are compliant, without ever handing over a single line of personal data.

fedBundle.title: Offline profile bundles
fedBundle.body: Industry and site profiles ship as offline tarballs with manifests and checksums; imports preview first, then admins apply to the fact base and audit trail. Identities and sensitive detail stay at the source.

roadmap.title: Roadmap 2026 - 2030
roadmap.subtitle: Each step comes with a milestone you can verify.
roadmap.y2026: Stable local fact base and complete audit trail
roadmap.y2027: On-device visual world models and cross-facility experience sharing
roadmap.y2028: Open home-brain interfaces, inviting a wider developer community
roadmap.y2029: A next-generation protocol for devices from different brands
roadmap.y2030: Helping local AI and zero-knowledge audit move toward industry standards

developers.title: Developers & integration
developers.subtitle: Stable APIs, a plugin ecosystem, open source kernel -- for developers and systems integrators.

cases.title: Pilot Program
cases.subtitle: We aren't publishing customer cases yet -- we are recruiting the first 3-5 pilot sites, and the real stories will come after that.
cases.heroTitle:
We are not
publishing cases yet.
cases.heroBody: AIOS has cleared engineering acceptance, but it has not yet run a full pilot inside a real site. Until it does, we would rather leave this page empty than fill it with a polished case page -- and instead use it to find the partners who want to run the first real one with us.
cases.fitTitle: What kind of site we are looking for
cases.fitSubtitle: Bigger isn't better -- the right shape is.
cases.fit1: Senior apartments, community-care centers, rehab support wards, or other high-responsibility private spaces
cases.fit2: A single floor or a single building, 20-200 beds or units, can run a 4-8 week pilot end to end
cases.fit3: One operations or care lead who can decide -- not a chain that has to escalate every step
cases.fit4: Permits the necessary network and device changes; OK with engineers on site
cases.provide1: Hardware and software during the pilot are subsidized or free, depending on the agreement
cases.provide2: Engineers on site to install, plus remote support that does not pull out before the pilot ends
cases.provide3: Acceptance KPIs and process defined together with you -- not handed down by us
cases.provide4: An honest written debrief at the end of the pilot, one copy each
cases.notProvide1: Will not replace your care staff, and will not replace your existing ticketing or scheduling
cases.notProvide2: Will not upload any video or audio to the cloud; data stays on site by default
cases.notProvide3: Will not put your name or your data in any external material without you signing off on it first
cases.promise: Once the first pilots are done, this page will carry the real customer stories -- with data both sides have signed off on, the KPIs as agreed, and the parts that fell short. Until then, this page only carries what we are looking for. No projections. No estimates.
cases.ctaSecondary: Or talk first to see if it fits

pilot.title: Apply for Pilot
pilot.subtitle: A 2 to 4 week pilot for a single room or floor. Validate, then scale.

contact.title: Contact Sales
contact.subtitle: Describe your scenario and scale. We respond within one business day.
contact.name: Your name
contact.ok: Received. We will contact you within one business day.

privacy.title: Privacy & Compliance
privacy.subtitle: We believe data belongs to the user, not to us.

labs.subtitle: Early explorations, archived. Not part of the main product line.
